lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha! I am Count Check, the renowned security expert. Would you like me to check your account to see if it is secure?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Check my account, Count Check!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You have a Jagex Account, and you have set a Bank PIN, so you pass my checks! As you've passed my check for the first time, I award you a prize.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You have a Jagex Account, and you have set a Bank PIN, so you pass my checks! As you've passed my check before, there's no prize now.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You do not have a Jagex Account, so you fail my checks! You should upgrade before we meet again.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You do not have a Jagex Account, nor a Bank PIN, so so you fail my checks! Please upgrade before we meet again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me any advice?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: I can! Count with the Count as I count the steps to securing your account:

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: ONE... unique password!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Never use the same password for different accounts, or on different websites. If it got leaked, all of your accounts would become vulnerable at once!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: TWO... step verification for your email account!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Good email providers let you set up two-step verification, maybe by generating security codes on a second device, or by sending codes to your phone. Make sure you've enabled this.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: THREE... Um... Use a Jagex Account. I can see you already do this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Carry on.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that got to do with THREE?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Look, I'd got something for ONE, TWO and FOUR, and I like counting, okay? Don't interrupt my flow!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: THREE... Um... Use a Jagex Account.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Visit the RuneScape website and log in to update your Account settings.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: FOUR... digit Bank PIN! I can see you have set one.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: FOUR... digit Bank PIN!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Next time you visit a bank, talk to a banker and ask to check your PIN settings.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: FOUR... digit Bank PIN! I can see you do not use banks, by the way.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Muhahahahhaa! Such fun it is to count!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I learn more about security?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: The Stronghold of Security, in Barbarian Village. I see you have not visited it. Would you like to? I can send you straight there - but only once.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: So may I check your account for you?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: The Stronghold of Security, in Barbarian Village. There is much to learn there. But while you are here, may I check your account for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: As you may see, I am a vampyre, from Morytania. I left there when I heard the terrible news and now I choose to spend my days warning others of the perils that lay in wait for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The perils?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Yes, be warned! The travellers of these lands face danger from outside. Accounts can be stolen, and when that happens one can lose everything one has worked for.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: I've left my vampyric ways behind me, and now I spend my days letting everyone know of this terrible danger.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: In return for my dedication, I have been blessed with the ability to check if adventurers are ready to defend themselves against these account thieves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're a vampyre, how did you cross the Salve?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: What do you mean? 'If' I am a vampyre? Of course I am a vampyre! See my pale complexion, my teeth, my outfit - how could anyone doubt I am a true vampyre?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: I used to stalk the night drinking blood like vampyres do, and I am certainly not just a role-playing enthusiast in a costume! Never question that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: So may I check your account for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you another time.

sections.title: Talking to him again as he's leaving

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: I must be leaving now.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: I came to check [player name], not you.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha! I, Count Check, have checked your account. You have a Jagex Account, and you have set a Bank PIN, so you pass my checks! Enjoy your prize.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha! I, Count Check, have checked your account. You do not have a Bank PIN, so you fail my checks! Talk to a banker to set a PIN before we meet again.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha! I, Count Check, have checked your account. You do not have a Jagex Account, so you fail my checks! You should upgrade before we meet again.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha! I, Count Check, have checked your account. You do not have a Jagex Account, nor a Bank PIN, so so you fail my checks! Please upgrade before we meet again.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Please let me check you, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: I have come to check you, [player name]...

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ahahahaha, ONE more person ignored me...

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Hello [player name] you look like you've seen a ghost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, not a ghost, but... well... you're a vampyre aren't you? Shouldn't you be in Morytania?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ah yes, I miss that place. So lovely and dark. I left there when I heard the terrible news and now I choose to spend the rest of my days warning others of the perils that lay in wait for them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Terrible news? Peril? I don't like the sound of this. Is there something I should know?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Yes be warned! The travellers of these lands face danger from outside. Accounts that allow them access to this magical place can be stolen and when that happens they could lose everything they have worked so hard

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: for. I've left my Vampyre ways behind me and now spend my days letting everyone know of this terrible danger. In return for my dedication I have been blessed with a unique ability to be able to check if

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: adventurers who speak with me are ready to defend themselves against these account thieves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you check to see if my account is secure?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Ok let me check your account security, but before I do there are some things that even I can not see. Firstly you must have a unique password for your account and not share it with anyone else and secondly make

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: sure you have 2 step verification for your registered recovery email. I'll leave it with you to make sure both of those things are in place.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A unique password that I don't share, and 2 step verification on my email... got it.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Now let me take a look at some of your other security settings for you...

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You are using the authenticator! Excellent. This will help to keep your account secure.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You aren't using the authenticator! This is a disaster. You should enable this to keep your account secure. Simply visit the support link on the oldschool.runescape.com website.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You have a bank PIN. This is good, but make sure that it's one that can't be easily guessed.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: You really should set a bank PIN. If your account does get stolen, your items in the bank and other places like the GE will be safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there an easy way to remember all these account security things?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Yes there is. by counting. I am a count, who likes to count and check accounts. You can ask me to check your account at any time you like and if you want to remember my top tips for account security just ask me

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: to count for you. The count loves to count.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Count with the count to easily remember my top account security tips:

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: One... unique password.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Two... step email verification.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Free... authenticator.

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Four... digit bank PIN!

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Muhahahahhaa!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there somewhere I can learn more about account security?

lines.speaker: Count Check
lines.text: Yes! The Stronghold of Security which can be accessed via Barbarian Village.
